title: senecio peltophorus
intro: Senecio peltophorus, also known as Devil's Bush or Stinking Nelly, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to Australia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to repel insects and as a treatment for various health issues. The roots and leaves of Senecio peltophorus are used in herbal preparations, which are said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions, and supporting respiratory health. The plant is often prepared as a tincture or tea, but due to its toxicity, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a medicinal treatment.
